working_definition: A monocyte-derived dendritic cell subtype (moDC_2) in adult human skin, identified by Reynolds et al. (2021) as an intermediate maturation state within the monocyte-derived DC differentiation branch. Positioned between moDC_1 (less mature) and moDC_3 (most mature, expressing maturation and migration genes), moDC_2 represents a transitional state acquiring dendritic cell identity while partially retaining monocyte-derived features.

best_match.cl_definition: A dendritic cell that is derived from a monocyte.

justification: CL:0011031 (monocyte-derived dendritic cell) is the parent term for all monocyte-derived DCs. moDC_2 is a specific subtype within this class defined by Reynolds et al. (2021) in adult skin, representing an intermediate maturation state. No CL subtype term exists for moDC_2 specifically.

other_candidates.cl_definition: A dendritic cell of the myeloid lineage.
other_candidates.reason_rejected: Too broad; moDCs are a specific subset within myeloid DCs distinguished by their monocyte origin

recommendation: Use CL:0011031 (monocyte-derived dendritic cell) as a broad match. No new CL term is needed since the subtypes moDC_1/2/3 are dataset-specific clusters along a maturation continuum.
